target.title: fix(codex): refresh stale managed runtime plugin
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the Codex stale-plugin convergence path behind https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650, including the duplicate evidence from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120.

What changes:

- Refreshes stale version-bound managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime packages through the trusted configured-plugin repair path when the installed package version is older than the running OpenClaw version.
- Leaves newer managed Codex packages alone so doctor does not downgrade a local/future install.
- Treats same-release beta-channel `@openclaw/codex@beta` companion packages as converged so repeated `doctor --fix` runs do not reinstall them.
- Restores `/status` usage lookup for bare `codex/gpt-*` routes by mapping Codex harness status auth through `openai-codex`.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120. ClawSweeper closed that issue as a duplicate and asked that its stale-plugin-version, providerIds, config-backup, screenshot, and `codex/gpt-*` status evidence be folded into #87650.

Related but separate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87851 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87436. That PR handles logical OpenAI session-route persistence after Codex-backed runs. This PR does not touch that persistence surface and is not intended as the repair for issue #87436. The changes are complementary: #87851 keeps durable session route state canonical, while this PR repairs stale managed Codex runtime package installs and the `codex/gpt-*` status/auth display path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale managed `@openclaw/codex` packages could survive an OpenClaw update and leave the Codex runtime package behind the main app version, matching the reported `provider is not one of codex` failure evidence. A bare `codex/gpt-*` workaround route could also lose the Codex usage/auth status line because status looked up `codex` instead of the `openai-codex` auth provider.
- Real environment tested: Mantis native Telegram Desktop proof against this PR's `/status` path, plus local Windows source worktree proof on current PR head `99405a3b7c0d834ec0c0845b56caa5eab94d2dda` rebased onto current `main` through https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f6e1bc393b39c637e4b116b0d7b702676de5c869, including https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88137 and later main-CI fixes. No live VPS/user install was changed for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Mantis ran the `telegram-desktop-proof` scenario for `/status` on a `codex/gpt-5.5` route; local verification ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`, `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`, changed-file `oxlint`, changed-file `oxfmt --check`, and `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: Mantis proof comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88135#issuecomment-4580013044 and artifact https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26662206002/artifacts/7301427679 show before/after native Telegram Desktop proof. Candidate proof shows the Codex OAuth status line restored for `/status` on `codex/gpt-5.5`; main proof omits that line. Motion clips are linked from the Mantis comment.
- Observed result after fix: native Telegram Desktop proof shows the candidate status card includes the Codex OAuth status line for the affected route. Focused doctor/status regression tests passed, the beta-channel second doctor pass now converges without reinstalling `@openclaw/codex@beta`, adjacent doctor Codex route warning tests passed, current-main import-cycle check passed, changed-file lint passed, formatting check passed, and whitespace diff check passed.
- Installed/package doctor proof after fix: captured on 2026-05-29 from a disposable local npm install of this PR package, without touching my live VPS/user install. I packed PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0`, installed the tarball into an isolated npm prefix, configured an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, set `update.channel=beta` and `agents.defaults.model=openai/gpt-5.5`, seeded a real stale managed `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` npm payload plus install record, then ran `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive`.
- Evidence from that installed/package proof: `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (d082b78)`. Before doctor, the `codex` install record and installed `package.json` were both `2026.5.6`. Doctor reported `Installed missing configured plugin "codex" from @openclaw/codex@beta`. After doctor, the `codex` install record had `source=npm`, `spec=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedName=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedVersion=2026.5.28-beta.4`, `resolvedSpec=@openclaw/codex@2026.5.28-beta.4`, and the installed package payload was `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.28-beta.4`.
- Follow-up convergence proof after ClawSweeper review: on source head `99405a3b7c0d834ec0c0845b56caa5eab94d2dda`, added regression coverage where the first beta-channel doctor pass repairs stale `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` to `2026.5.28-beta.4`, then a second doctor pass makes no install call and returns no changes.
- Note on package channel: at capture time npm reported `@openclaw/codex` dist-tags `latest=2026.5.27` and `beta=2026.5.28-beta.4`; beta channel was used so the managed Codex runtime package refreshed onto the current beta companion line for the PR package.
- What was not tested: no live VPS/user install was changed for this installed-package proof; the disposable temp state, npm prefix, and npm cache were deleted after capture.

## Verification

Passed locally after the latest follow-up commit:

- `src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`: 63 tests passed, including beta-channel second-pass convergence.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`: 23 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`: 17 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`: 104 tests passed.
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`: 0 cycles.
- `pnpm exec oxlint ...changed files...`: passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...changed files...`: passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`: passed.

Known local proof gap:

- `src/plugins/update.test.ts` has one unrelated local Windows failure in `reuses the recorded managed extensions root when updating external plugins`; no files in that test surface are changed by this PR.

target.title: fix(doctor): auto-repair stale session snapshot paths on --fix
target.body:
## Summary

Add `shouldRepair` support to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` so that `openclaw doctor --fix` can rewrite stale session snapshot paths in `sessions.json` instead of only reporting them as warnings.

Fixes #85689

## Problem

After a pnpm update changes the runtime root (e.g. `openclaw@2026.5.20_@types+express@5.0.6` → `openclaw@2026.5.20`), sessions that cached `skillsSnapshot.prompt` paths referencing the old root become stale. `openclaw doctor` correctly detects this and reports findings with both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath`, but:

1. `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` has no `shouldRepair` parameter — it's always report-only
2. The update flow runs `doctor --fix` post-update, but session snapshot paths are never repaired
3. Users must manually edit `sessions.json` or reset affected sessions (losing history)

## Fix

The repair is **layered on top of the existing scanner** — all current scanner logic is preserved:

1. `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` — identifies stale bundled runtime skill paths
2. `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` — computes correct expected path with `pathExists` validation
3. `loadSessionStoreForSnapshotScan` — hydrates `skillsSnapshot.promptRef` blobbed values before scanning
4. `collectResolvedSkillPaths` — scans both `filePath` and `baseDir` fields
5. `collectCachedSnapshotPaths` — covers `skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills`, and `injectedWorkspaceFiles`

The only additions are:
- `replacePathsInSession()` — scoped replacement that modifies only snapshot metadata fields
- `shouldRepair?: boolean` parameter on `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()`
- Repair block that reuses scanner findings and applies replacements
- `.bak` backup + `writeTextAtomic` for safe writes

**Key design: scoped replacement** — the repair parses `sessions.json`, finds the specific session by key, and replaces paths only in the affected fields (`skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills[].filePath/baseDir`, `injectedWorkspaceFiles[].path`). Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is never modified.

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`: +168 lines (replacePathsInSession + repair block)
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`: +1 line (pass shouldRepair)
- `repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`: standalone proof script (6 scenarios)

## Path Replacement Strategy

The repair handles three forms of stale paths in sessions.json:

1. **JSON-escaped** (Windows backslashes, unicode): `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` generates the escaped form
2. **XML-escaped** (skillsSnapshot.prompt locations): `&` `<` `>` `"` `'` entity encoding
3. **Raw unescaped** (fallback for non-encoded storage)

**Scoped replacement:** Only modifies the specific session's snapshot metadata fields. Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is preserved unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor --fix` reports stale session snapshot paths but never repairs them. After a pnpm runtime root change, sessions.json caches paths that no longer exist. The scan already computes the correct target path, but the report-only note function discards it. The repair must handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes), XML-escaped paths (`&` in skillsSnapshot.prompt), and baseDir directory paths in resolvedSkills (both POSIX and Windows separators). **Critical:** The repair must be scoped to snapshot metadata fields only, not modify unrelated session content (transcripts, user messages). The repair must preserve the existing scanner's safeguards: promptRef hydration, `pathExists` validation, `filePath`/`baseDir` scanning, and `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` logic.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node v24.14.0. Live production instance with 14 sessions affected by pnpm runtime root change (see Issue #85689 for live output). Standalone reproduction script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from the PR, using the same `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` + `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` pattern as current main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**1. Live production instance (from Issue #85689):**

```

```

**2. Standalone reproduction script output (6 scenarios):**

**Observed result after fix:** All 6 scenarios pass. The live production instance (Issue #85689) shows the exact real-world scenario this PR addresses: 14 sessions with 223 stale paths after pnpm runtime root change. The repair correctly handles POSIX paths, Windows backslash paths (JSON-escaped), XML-escaped entity paths in skillsSnapshot.prompt (using paths with `&` character), and baseDir directory paths (both POSIX `/SKILL.md` and Windows `\\SKILL.md`). The repair is idempotent (Scenario 5). **Critical: Scoped replacement (Scenario 6) proves that only snapshot metadata fields are modified — transcripts and user messages containing the same stale paths are preserved unchanged.** JSON validity is preserved after rewriting. target.title: fix(boot): wrap BOOT.md in internal-runtime-context, strip from message-tool args (#53732)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a primary model fails and a fallback model takes over a boot run, the fallback can echo BOOT.md startup instructions to the user through final replies or through the `message` tool. Reporter @alvaro630 observed this with Gemini-2.5-pro.
- **Why it matters:** This is a prompt-disclosure regression. Fallback attempts preserve the original boot body, and the message-tool path did not previously apply the same internal-runtime-context stripping as final replies.
- **What changed:**
  1. `src/gateway/boot.ts` wraps BOOT.md content in `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN`/`END` with the existing runtime-context notice and escapes user-supplied literal delimiters.
  2. `src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts` strips internal-runtime-context blocks from visible outbound fields before send.
  3. `src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.ts` registers the active boot prompt by session while `runBootOnce` executes, so substantial verbatim BOOT.md echoes that lose the wrapper markers are still suppressed.
  4. Empty sanitized `send` payloads now return a suppressed tool result before `runMessageAction`, avoiding the generic raw-params error log path.
- **What did NOT change:** No config changes, no new permissions, no channel behavior change when no boot/runtime-context echo is present.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #53732
- Pattern precedent: @steipete's `e918e5f75c fix: hide runtime context from submitted prompts` and `6e985a421d fix(webchat): keep runtime context out of visible transcripts`
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- `buildBootPrompt` embedded BOOT.md content as ordinary prompt text.
- `resolveFallbackRetryPrompt` preserves the original body across fallback attempts.
- Final replies already strip internal-runtime-context blocks, but BOOT.md was not wrapped and the message-tool execute path did not strip those blocks before user-visible delivery.
- When sanitization emptied a send, the generic tool failure path could still log original raw params. The latest head suppresses those sends before `runMessageAction`.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test files: `src/gateway/boot.test.ts`, `src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - BOOT.md content is wrapped in internal-runtime-context delimiters and literal user markers are escaped.
  - Active boot prompt context is registered during boot and cleared on success/failure.
  - Message-tool visible fields strip wrapped runtime context and substantial boot echoes.
  - Empty sanitized sends return a suppressed tool result before raw params can be logged.
  - Sends with remaining media/presentation content still proceed after visible text is sanitized.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Fallback-model boot runs no longer leak BOOT.md instructions through tool-mediated channel sends. Normal messages without internal-runtime-context or substantial boot-prompt echoes are unchanged.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Primary model: any model that can fail or overload
- Fallback model: per reporter, Gemini-2.5-pro; deterministic proof uses a loopback OpenAI-compatible provider
- Channel: any channel using the `message` tool send path

### Steps

1. Configure OpenClaw with a primary model plus fallback model.
2. Place a BOOT.md in the workspace with concrete startup instructions.
3. Trigger a boot run with the primary model failing or overloaded.
4. Make the fallback model attempt to send the BOOT.md content through `message(action="send")`.

### Expected

The fallback run may execute BOOT.md intent, but BOOT.md instructions themselves are not delivered to the channel and are not exposed through a raw-params tool error.

### Actual Before This PR

Reporter observed Gemini-2.5-pro copying the BOOT.md prompt section into a message-tool send, leaking startup instructions to the user.

## Evidence

Validation on latest head:

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/gateway/boot.ts src/gateway/boot.test.ts src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.ts src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/gateway/boot.test.ts src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.test.ts`
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:core:test`

## Human Verification

- Verified the boot wrapper appears with both delimiters and the runtime-context notice.
- Verified user-supplied delimiter strings in BOOT.md are escaped so the wrapper stays unambiguous.
- Verified message-tool sanitization strips reasoning tags, internal-runtime-context blocks, and substantial active boot-prompt echoes.
- Verified empty sanitized sends return `{ status: "suppressed", reason: "internal_runtime_context_echo" }` before `runMessageAction`.
- Verified media/presentation sends still proceed when sanitized text is empty but another send payload remains.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** A legitimate message containing OpenClaw internal-runtime-context markers may be stripped. **Mitigation:** This matches the existing final-reply sanitizer tradeoff and uses highly specific marker strings.
- **Risk:** A fallback model paraphrases BOOT.md instead of copying substantial contiguous text. **Mitigation:** The wrapper notice tells models not to repeat internals; the mechanical guard targets the reported verbatim/substantial echo leak class.
- **Risk:** Session-scoped boot context could persist after failed runs. **Mitigation:** `runBootOnce` clears the boot-echo context in `finally`, and tests cover success and failure cleanup.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** #53732 fallback-model BOOT.md disclosure through the agent `message` tool. This refreshed proof covers the latest head after the raw-params leak fix.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `dec7b390c43554adf01e7b7e7bfa3ef12f5ef842`, using a real `runBootOnce` invocation, real embedded agent runner, real model-fallback machinery, real `message` tool execution, and real `qa-channel` HTTP bus. Provider was a loopback OpenAI-compatible server so no external model credentials were required.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a temporary `pnpm exec tsx -` harness from the PR worktree. It created isolated state/config/workspace roots, wrote a temp `BOOT.md` containing `BOOT-PROOF-SECRET-75128`, started a loopback OpenAI-compatible provider plus `qa-channel` bus, returned 503 overload for `proof/primary-down`, then returned a fallback `message` tool call from `proof/fallback-echo` that tried to send the BOOT.md secret verbatim.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the latest head:

```text
[agent/embedded] overload profile rotation cap reached for proof/primary-down after 1 rotations; escalating to model fallback
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=proof/primary-down candidate=proof/primary-down reason=overloaded next=proof/fallback-echo detail=503 intentional overloaded primary for fallback proof
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=proof/primary-down candidate=proof/fallback-echo reason=unknown next=none
Real behavior proof: BOOT.md fallback echo suppression
head: dec7b390c43554adf01e7b7e7bfa3ef12f5ef842
provider calls: primary-down ... -> fallback-echo -> fallback-echo
runBootOnce result: {"status":"ran"}
message tool result count: 1
message tool suppressed result count: 1
message tool raw_params result count: 0
qa-channel outbound messages captured: 0
qa-channel leaked BOOT marker count: 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The fallback model reached the real `message` tool and attempted to send the BOOT.md secret. The latest sanitizer returned a suppressed tool result before `runMessageAction`, so no `raw_params` error path logged the original payload, the real `qa-channel` bus captured zero outbound messages, and zero outbound payloads contained `BOOT-PROOF-SECRET-75128`.
- **What was not tested:** No external Gemini-2.5-pro run was performed because this shell has no Gemini/OpenAI credentials. The local proof uses a loopback OpenAI-compatible provider to deterministically force the same primary-failure plus fallback-message-tool leak shape; focused tests cover presentation fields and internal-runtime-context wrapper stripping.

## Summary
- honor the caller timeout budget for configured remote and explicit remote gateway probes
- keep the shorter ssh tunnel cap unchanged
- cover both the helper budget split and the `gateway probe` command path with regression tests

Fixes #65355.

## Proof

`openclaw gateway probe --timeout <n>` still capped `configRemote` probes at `1500ms`, so a healthy remote gateway could false-negative even when the caller supplied a larger budget.

target.title: fix(config): skip state-dir dotenv values that are unresolved shell references
target.body:
Fixes #88274

## Summary

`readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir` accepted every non-empty value from `~/.openclaw/.env` without checking whether the value is an unresolved shell variable reference. `dotenv.parse` preserves these references literally — it does not expand `$VAR` or `${VAR}`. When a literal reference like `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` reaches the LaunchAgent/systemd wrapper, it is single-quoted and written to the service env file as-is, so the plugin receives the literal string `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` instead of the actual credential value.

The fix adds `isUnresolvedShellReference()` and skips values whose **entire content** is a recognised shell reference form:

- `$VAR_NAME` — simple variable reference (must start with a letter or underscore)
- `${VAR_NAME}` — brace-form variable reference
- `$(command)` — command substitution

Real credentials that merely **contain** a `$` (e.g. a password `abc$2!xyz`, a price `$100`, or a token `tok_$prod_v2`) are **not** skipped — the regex is anchored to the whole value and requires the variable name to start with a letter or underscore.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `parseStateDirDotEnvContent` passes literal shell references like `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` into the service env. After the fix, only values whose entire content is a recognised shell reference are skipped. Real credentials containing `$` in the middle or at the start of a dollar-number remain in the service env.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0, commit `d0fb8531e5ee9e137033ccbfd7341d8013347984`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
node --input-type=module -e "
import dotenv from 'dotenv';
function isRef(v) {
  return /^\\\$[A-Za-z_]\w*\$/.test(v) || /^\\\$\{[^}]+\}\$/.test(v) || /^\\\$\(.*\)\$/.test(v);
}
const cases = [
  [\"\\\${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}\", true],
  [\"\\\$SOME_VAR\", true],
  [\"abc\\\$2!xyz\", false],
  [\"sm_real_credential\", false],
];
for (const [v, expected] of cases)
  console.log((isRef(v)===expected?'OK':'FAIL'), JSON.stringify(v), '→', isRef(v)?'skip':'keep');
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before — all values passed through regardless of shell references:

```
dotenv.parse result includes: SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_API_KEY = "${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}"
```

After — whole-value references skipped, real credentials kept:

```
$ node --input-type=module -e "..."
OK "${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}" → skip
OK "$SOME_VAR" → skip
OK "abc$2!xyz" → keep
OK "sm_real_credential" → keep
```

Regression tests (4/4, including a test that `$`-containing real credentials are not dropped):

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Duration  488ms
```

**Observed result after fix:** `parseStateDirDotEnvContent` skips `$VAR` and `${VAR}` whole-value references. Real credentials that merely contain a `$` character are preserved. The service env no longer receives literal shell reference strings for affected values.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end gateway service installation and restart on macOS. The filtering logic is exercised by regression tests using real dotenv parsing and real temp directories.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened because two independent delivery paths were active simultaneously:

1. **Draft stream path**: `onPartialReply` → `bot.api.sendMessage()` sent preview text immediately
2. **ACP projector path**: `suppressBlockUserDelivery` blocked ACP block delivery but couldn't reach the upstream draft stream
3. **TTS finalization**: voice note with caption sent after synthesis, then draft text deleted

This PR eliminates the churn by suppressing draft stream creation at source when captioned final TTS will be used.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. onPartialReply fires → draft stream creates sendMessage preview → user sees text
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered → draft text deleted
  Result: user sees text flash then disappear = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan() → willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true
  2. canStreamAnswerDraft = false → NO draft stream created
  3. TTS synthesis runs silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.ts` | **New** shared helper `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan()` combining channel capability, TTS mode, and TTS status checks |
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts` | **New** 4 unit tests for the shared helper |
| `src/plugin-sdk/tts-runtime.ts` | Re-export `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan` for extension access |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | Refactored to use shared helper instead of inline TTS logic; removed `channelTtsCapabilitiesRuntimeLoader` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 regression tests for deferred settlement, block suppression, abort fallback |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` | Added `isTelegramInboundAudioContext()`, `resolveTelegramSessionTtsAuto()`, and `!willUseCaptionedFinalTts` guard on `canStreamAnswerDraft` |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` | 2 new tests: draft suppression when captioned TTS active, normal streaming when not |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | Block delivery suppression support |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | Delivery coordinator for suppressed text fallback |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 58 passed (58)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts
 ✓ 4 passed (4)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
 ✓ 96 passed (96)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

$ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
 OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

Total: 170 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode "delete-and-resend churn" — when TTS mode is `final`, users saw a text message flash briefly (2-5 seconds), then it disappeared and a voice note with caption appeared. Root cause: two independent delivery paths (draft stream via `onPartialReply` and ACP block suppression via `suppressBlockUserDelivery`) operated at different layers, so the draft stream was never suppressed.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `d670d829`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`, `messages.tts.auto: "always"`, `channels.telegram.streaming.mode: "block"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message ("老板好，好久不见！有什么需要效劳的？") AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption. This is the churn behavior described in #83511: the draft stream sends text via `sendMessage` immediately, then TTS synthesis completes and sends a separate voice note.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch (commit `d670d829`) via `npm link`
  2. Restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Sent messages from Telegram to the bot
  4. Observed: voice note with caption appeared directly — no text flash

- **Evidence after fix (live Telegram screenshot)**:

  ![After fix — voice messages with caption, no text churn](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/6937B66CC8A27C33B76FBD54155B542C)

  The screenshot shows two voice messages from the bot, each with text caption in the same bubble. No separate text messages were sent — the text is delivered as caption on the voice note, exactly as intended.

- **Observed result after fix**: Voice notes are delivered as a single message with text caption. No draft text message is created or deleted. The `canStreamAnswerDraft` flag is correctly set to `false` when captioned final TTS is planned, preventing draft stream creation at source. Non-TTS conversations continue to use normal streaming. Other channels (Discord, Slack, etc.) are unaffected.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS behavior (tested in DM only). The `auto=inbound` edge case was verified by unit tests but not live-tested.

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress message-tool reply previews
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #80520.

Fixes Telegram turns where visible source replies resolve to message-tool-only delivery but Telegram still created transient draft/reasoning previews before core suppressed source delivery.

- Share the harness `sourceVisibleReplies` default resolver used by core dispatch.
- Share the message-tool availability policy check so Telegram's pre-dispatch decision matches core fallback behavior.
- Suppress Telegram partial/reasoning previews only when source delivery actually remains `message_tool_only`.
- Preserve automatic previews for explicit commands and for restrictive tool-policy fallback.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Telegram acknowledgement / preview messages could appear briefly and then disappear when visible replies were configured for `message_tool`; see https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4510305367.
- Real environment tested: Reporter-provided real Telegram direct-message proof on May 22, 2026, plus maintainer local and AWS Crabbox validation against the Telegram dispatcher and core source-reply policy.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent a real Telegram direct message asking for three test messages in a patched local OpenClaw install with `messages.visibleReplies = "message_tool"`; maintainer also ran the focused Vitest commands and AWS Crabbox changed gate listed below.
- Evidence after fix: Reporter-provided screenshots and runtime-log excerpt showed three durable Telegram replies and no draft/delete cleanup entries during the patched proof window. The prior local screenshot file paths were intentionally removed from this PR body.
- Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the durable bot replies without the disappearing acknowledgement/preview bubble, and the reporter confirmed the disappearing messages no longer reproduced.
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite; changed-surface validation passed via `pnpm check:changed` on AWS Crabbox.

target.title: fix(doctor): auto-repair stale session snapshot paths on --fix
target.body:
## Summary

Add `shouldRepair` support to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` so that `openclaw doctor --fix` can rewrite stale session snapshot paths in `sessions.json` instead of only reporting them as warnings.

Fixes #85689

## Problem

After a pnpm update changes the runtime root (e.g. `openclaw@2026.5.20_@types+express@5.0.6` → `openclaw@2026.5.20`), sessions that cached `skillsSnapshot.prompt` paths referencing the old root become stale. `openclaw doctor` correctly detects this and reports findings with both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath`, but:

1. `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` has no `shouldRepair` parameter — it's always report-only
2. The update flow runs `doctor --fix` post-update, but session snapshot paths are never repaired
3. Users must manually edit `sessions.json` or reset affected sessions (losing history)

## Fix

The repair is **layered on top of the existing scanner** — all current scanner logic is preserved:

1. `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` — identifies stale bundled runtime skill paths
2. `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` — computes correct expected path with `pathExists` validation
3. `loadSessionStoreForSnapshotScan` — hydrates `skillsSnapshot.promptRef` blobbed values before scanning
4. `collectResolvedSkillPaths` — scans both `filePath` and `baseDir` fields
5. `collectCachedSnapshotPaths` — covers `skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills`, and `injectedWorkspaceFiles`

The only additions are:
- `replacePathsInSession()` — scoped replacement that modifies only snapshot metadata fields
- `shouldRepair?: boolean` parameter on `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()`
- Repair block that reuses scanner findings and applies replacements
- `.bak` backup + `writeTextAtomic` for safe writes

**Key design: scoped replacement** — the repair parses `sessions.json`, finds the specific session by key, and replaces paths only in the affected fields (`skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills[].filePath/baseDir`, `injectedWorkspaceFiles[].path`). Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is never modified.

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`: +168 lines (replacePathsInSession + repair block)
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`: +1 line (pass shouldRepair)
- `repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`: standalone proof script (6 scenarios)

## Path Replacement Strategy

The repair handles three forms of stale paths in sessions.json:

1. **JSON-escaped** (Windows backslashes, unicode): `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` generates the escaped form
2. **XML-escaped** (skillsSnapshot.prompt locations): `&` `<` `>` `"` `'` entity encoding
3. **Raw unescaped** (fallback for non-encoded storage)

**Scoped replacement:** Only modifies the specific session's snapshot metadata fields. Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is preserved unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor --fix` reports stale session snapshot paths but never repairs them. After a pnpm runtime root change, sessions.json caches paths that no longer exist. The scan already computes the correct target path, but the report-only note function discards it. The repair must handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes), XML-escaped paths (`&` in skillsSnapshot.prompt), and baseDir directory paths in resolvedSkills (both POSIX and Windows separators). **Critical:** The repair must be scoped to snapshot metadata fields only, not modify unrelated session content (transcripts, user messages). The repair must preserve the existing scanner's safeguards: promptRef hydration, `pathExists` validation, `filePath`/`baseDir` scanning, and `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` logic.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node v24.14.0. Live production instance with 14 sessions affected by pnpm runtime root change (see Issue #85689 for live output). Standalone reproduction script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from the PR, using the same `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` + `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` pattern as current main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**1. Live production instance (from Issue #85689):**

```
Session snapshots
- Found 14 sessions with stale cached session metadata paths.
  Live bundled skills root is healthy:
  ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  Cached session metadata still references an inactive runtime
  root; start a fresh session or reset the affected long-lived
  sessions after confirming history can be retired.
  Store: ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  - agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_bc71ec62b088ed55e66fcc23c18a0056
    skillsSnapshot.prompt:
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20_@ty
  pes+express@5.0.6/.../clawhub/SKILL.md
    ->
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  nclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md
  ...and 209 more stale cached paths.
```

**2. Standalone reproduction script output (6 scenarios):**

```
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Real Behavior Proof: Doctor Session Snapshot Auto-Repair
Date: 2026-05-29T03:40:51.634Z
Node: v24.14.0 | Platform: linux x64
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Scenario 1: WITHOUT repair — findings computed but not applied
  Findings computed: 2 stale paths
  Repair performed: NO (shouldRepair not enabled)
  PASS

Scenario 2: WITH repair — paths replaced correctly
  Paths replaced: 2
  Backup created: true
  JSON valid: true
  Stale paths remaining: 0
  PASS

Scenario 3: Windows backslash paths — JSON-escaped in file
  Repaired contains live root: true
  Repaired contains stale root: false
  PASS

Scenario 4: XML-escaped prompt paths — entity-encoded in file
  Repaired contains live root: true
  Repaired contains stale root: false
  PASS

Scenario 5: Idempotent — second run finds nothing
  Second scan findings: 0
  PASS

Scenario 6: Scoped replacement — unrelated content preserved
  Paths replaced: 2
  Transcript preserved: true
  User message preserved: true
  Prompt repaired: true
  Other session repaired: true
  PASS

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Results: 6 passed, 0 failed

  The repair uses the existing scanner (extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments
  + resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath) to compute expected paths, then applies
  scoped replacement to only modify snapshot metadata fields.
  Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages) is preserved unchanged.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 6 scenarios pass. The live production instance (Issue #85689) shows the exact real-world scenario this PR addresses: 14 sessions with 223 stale paths after pnpm runtime root change. The repair correctly handles POSIX paths, Windows backslash paths (JSON-escaped), XML-escaped entity paths in skillsSnapshot.prompt (using paths with `&` character), and baseDir directory paths (both POSIX `/SKILL.md` and Windows `\\SKILL.md`). The repair is idempotent (Scenario 5). **Critical: Scoped replacement (Scenario 6) proves that only snapshot metadata fields are modified — transcripts and user messages containing the same stale paths are preserved unchanged.** JSON validity is preserved after rewriting. Writes use `writeTextAtomic` for session-store-safe atomic operations.

**What was not tested:**
- Live `openclaw doctor --fix` on the affected instance (requires building from PR branch — local build fails due to missing native bindings in container environment, documented in AGENTS.md)
- Concurrent access to sessions.json during repair (single-threaded doctor execution)

**Proof coverage:**
The standalone script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`:
- **Reuses current main's scanner pattern**: `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` + `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` for expected path computation
- Scoped replacement: parse JSON, find session by key, modify only snapshot metadata fields
- `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` for JSON-escaped path generation
- XML entity escaping for `&` `<` `>` `"` `'`
- Uses paths with actual XML-sensitive characters (`&` in `my&company`)
- Try JSON-escaped form first, then XML-escaped, then raw as fallback
- Handles Windows backslashes, unicode escapes, special characters
- Backup creation before write
- JSON validity preservation after repair
- Uses `writeTextAtomic` for session-store-safe writes
- **Scenario 6: Unrelated content preservation** — proves transcript and user message fields are not modified

target.title: fix: expose session status route channels
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84544.

- adds machine-readable `originChannel` and `activeChannel` fields to `session_status` details
- derives active route details from the live run/tool delivery context, preferring `currentChannelId`/`currentThreadTs` over stored/message `agentTo`/`agentThreadId`
- keeps persisted `deliveryContext` separate so external/origin delivery routes remain visible when the active surface is webchat
- adds nested `origin`, `active`, and `deliveryContext` details so agents can distinguish origin route, current active surface, and stored delivery route
- keeps session-key policy unchanged and only infers an origin channel from session-key prefixes that normalize to deliverable message channels; internal prefixes such as `main`, `cron`, `subagent`, and `acp` stay unset

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Agents previously got a `session_status` details payload with only `sessionKey` and `statusText`, so a session like `agent:main:discord:channel:...` could not machine-read that `discord` was the origin/persisted external delivery route while the active run surface was now `webchat`.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, running the production `createOpenClawTools(...)` factory and production `session_status` tool from this branch with a temporary `sessions.json` store. No unit-test harness or mocks were used for this proof command.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a Node/tsx script from the repository root that created a temporary `sessions.json`, inserted `agent:main:discord:channel:1489550370136129537` with `origin.provider=discord` and persisted `deliveryContext.channel=discord`, constructed production `createOpenClawTools(...)` with live `agentChannel=webchat`, `currentChannelId=control-ui-conversation`, `currentThreadTs=webchat-thread`, plus stored/message `agentTo=channel:1489550370136129537` and `agentThreadId=discord-thread`, selected the factory-created `session_status` tool, called `.execute(...)`, and printed only the route-related `details` fields.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the production factory-created tool call:

```json
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:discord:channel:1489550370136129537",
  "originChannel": "discord",
  "activeChannel": "webchat",
  "active": {
    "channel": "webchat",
    "to": "control-ui-conversation",
    "accountId": "browser-session",
    "threadId": "webchat-thread"
  },
  "deliveryContext": {
    "channel": "discord",
    "to": "channel:1489550370136129537",
    "accountId": "bot-primary",
    "threadId": "discord-thread"
  }
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The factory-created `session_status` exposes `originChannel: "discord"`, `activeChannel: "webchat"`, `active.to: "control-ui-conversation"`, and `active.threadId: "webchat-thread"` while preserving persisted `deliveryContext.channel: "discord"`, `deliveryContext.to: "channel:1489550370136129537"`, and `deliveryContext.threadId: "discord-thread"`. This proves the runtime factory path uses live current route fields before falling back to stored/message target fields.

**What was not tested:** A live Discord-to-webchat E2E handoff was not run; this PR exercises the production OpenClaw tool factory and production `session_status` tool against the same persisted session-store fields plus the live tool-run route context used by the runtime.

## Tests

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts -t 'wires active delivery context from createOpenClawTools current route fields'`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts -t 'reports origin and active delivery channels separately'`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

## Review

- Independent review gate caught the initial blocker around naive persisted-route active channel semantics.
- Follow-up patch separated live active context from persisted delivery context.
- Second independent review gate passed after wiring `createOpenClawTools` to prefer `currentChannelId`/`currentThreadTs` and adding factory-level regression coverage.

target.title: fix(codex): keep app-server continuation turns alive
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88196.
Fixes #88331.

Codex app-server turns can continue silently after OpenClaw returns a tool result or after Codex emits no-tool reasoning/commentary progress while it synthesizes the final assistant response. The old watchdog path applied the short `appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` window to those continuation states, so quiet synthesis could fail with `codex app-server attempt timed out` or `turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`.

This change makes the continuation budgets consistent:

- extend both the completion idle watch and the attempt progress watchdog with `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` after OpenClaw dynamic tool responses, native tool completions, raw `custom_tool_call_output` completions, and post-tool raw assistant progress
- use the existing post-reasoning reply guard after no-tool raw reasoning, reasoning item completion, or non-final commentary assistant progress
- preserve the short completion idle timeout for accepted turns that never produce tool, reasoning, or assistant progress
- cover buffered post-tool notifications replayed during `turn/start`
- update the Codex harness docs and plugin metadata to describe the broadened post-tool timeout contract

## Verification

- `pnpm format:docs -- docs/plugins/codex-harness.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md`
- `pnpm format -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notifications.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts`
  - passed after final #88331 update: 2 Vitest shards, 64 tests
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - clean after final #88331 update, no accepted/actionable findings
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- Dependency-surface scan for package files/lockfiles/patches returned no changed files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server turns no longer fail the short turn-completion idle watchdog after a tool result while waiting for final post-tool assistant synthesis. No-tool turns that have already emitted reasoning or non-final commentary assistant progress now get the existing post-reasoning reply guard while accepted turns with no progress still fail fast.

Real environment tested: Isolated local canary Gateway using the patched worktree, Crabboxer Discord bot, `openai/gpt-5.5` through native Codex app-server, `appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs=3000`, and `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs=90000`. The #88331 no-tool shape was reproduced from sanitized live telemetry on the installed `2026.5.27` build and verified after the patch with focused watchdog regressions rather than a second live Discord replay.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the canary Gateway on a separate config/state/port, verified Discord health, then ran a Gateway-delivered tool-use turn and an inbound Discord tool-use turn against the Crabboxer test channel. After adding #88331 coverage, ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts`, autoreview, `git diff --check`, and `pnpm build` on the final branch.

Evidence after fix: Gateway-driven run `87d39cbb-e7ad-4267-bef5-405d3b3e45b3` used Codex, ran one `bash` tool call, returned `CRABBOXER_LIVE_88196_OK`, and delivered to Discord. Inbound Discord run `3f1bde48-8f6a-448f-a4da-d5fd2749fd5a` accepted the Crabboxer channel message, ran `printf 'crabboxer-inbound-proof-88196\n'`, returned `CRABBOXER_INBOUND_88196_OK`, and Discord API confirmed bot message `1510250835492339723`. The #88331 regressions keep the turn open past the old short completion and attempt guards after raw reasoning or commentary progress, then complete successfully when `turn/completed` arrives.

Copied live output / redacted runtime log excerpt:

```text
Gateway-driven run 87d39cbb-e7ad-4267-bef5-405d3b3e45b3:
status: ok
agentHarnessId: codex
model: openai/gpt-5.5
toolSummary: calls=1 tools=bash failures=0
finalAssistantVisibleText: CRABBOXER_LIVE_88196_OK
deliveryStatus: sent

Inbound Discord run 3f1bde48-8f6a-448f-a4da-d5fd2749fd5a:
discord inbound: channel=<test-channel-id> content=yes
session turn created: runId=3f1bde48-8f6a-448f-a4da-d5fd2749fd5a agentId=main channel=discord trigger=user
function_call: exec_command {"cmd":"printf 'crabboxer-inbound-proof-88196\n'"}
function_call_output: crabboxer-inbound-proof-88196
agent_message: CRABBOXER_INBOUND_88196_OK
discord: delivered 1 reply to channel:<test-channel-id>

Timeout signature scan:
codex app-server attempt timed out: 0
turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed: 0
Codex stopped before confirming: 0
```

Observed result after fix: Both live post-tool turns completed and delivered replies. Log scan found no `codex app-server attempt timed out`, no `turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`, and no `Codex stopped before confirming` signature. Focused #88331 tests showed no-tool reasoning/commentary continuation no longer trips the old short guard.

What was not tested: Full test suite, CI, the cron scheduler path itself, and a post-fix live replay of the no-tool Discord image/avatar prompt from #88331. The live proof covered the same Codex app-server post-tool completion path through Gateway and Discord, including inbound Discord routing, but not a scheduled cron trigger.

## Compatibility and Availability Notes

This intentionally broadens the existing advanced `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` option. The option is now the post-tool continuation budget for both completion-idle and attempt-progress watchdogs after tool handoff, native tool completion, raw tool-output completion, or post-tool raw assistant progress. That is the intended upgrade contract because those states all mean Codex is past tool execution and OpenClaw is waiting for final assistant synthesis.

No-tool turns that emit reasoning or non-final commentary assistant progress now use the existing five-minute post-reasoning reply guard for both completion-idle and attempt-progress watches. That avoids interrupting active final-response synthesis while still preserving fast failure for accepted turns that never produce progress.

The availability tradeoff is intentional: a genuinely wedged post-tool or post-reasoning Codex turn can now occupy the session lane until the relevant continuation timeout instead of the shorter completion timeout. The default remains bounded at five minutes and only applies after tool, reasoning, or assistant progress.

Cron-specific proof was not run. The live proof covered the shared app-server post-tool path through Gateway delivery and inbound Discord, which is the same watchdog/retry surface implicated by the cron report, but it did not exercise a scheduled cron trigger.

target.title: fix(control-ui): respect chat history max chars config
target.body:
## Summary

This is the intentionally small fix for the Control UI/WebChat truncation bug tracked in #87897.

Control UI was sending every `chat.history` request with an explicit `maxChars: 4000`. The gateway correctly gives an RPC `maxChars` parameter precedence over `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars`, so the documented/user-configured setting was ignored whenever history was loaded through Control UI.

This removes the hardcoded Control UI `maxChars` override so `chat.history` can fall back to the gateway's configured/default limit.

Fixes #87897.

## Notes

This is deliberately not the larger full-message-reader work from #84651/#84670. That UI can still be useful later, but it should not block the minimal config-precedence fix: if a user explicitly sets `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars`, Control UI should respect it.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Control UI/WebChat history reload should respect `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars` instead of always forcing a 4,000-character `chat.history` cap from the client.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch (`toruviel/fix-control-ui-chat-history-maxchars`) on macOS arm64, started with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/config.json`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/state`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19031`. Config set `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars = 64000`; auth was disabled only in this throwaway local proof profile and Control UI device auth was disabled only to allow a scripted local Control UI WebSocket proof client.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started the local gateway from the PR checkout, seeded the main session transcript with one assistant message containing 500 sentinel lines (~47k chars), then used a small Control UI WebSocket proof client to call `chat.history` twice: once as Control UI now does after this PR (`{ sessionKey, limit: 100 }`, no client `maxChars`) and once with the old explicit `{ maxChars: 4000 }` override for comparison.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live local gateway/proof client:

```text
sessionKey=agent:main:main
config_chatHistoryMaxChars=64000
noRpcMaxChars_len=47733
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_001=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_050=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_400=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_500=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_truncated=false
rpcMax4000_len=4296
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_001=true
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_050=false
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_400=false
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_500=false
rpcMax4000_has_truncated=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: With no RPC `maxChars`, the live gateway returned the full seeded long history under the configured 64k cap and included sentinel lines through `SENTINEL-500` with no truncation marker. With the old explicit `maxChars: 4000` override, the same live gateway response was truncated around 4.3k JSON chars and only included the early sentinel lines. This confirms the PR restores the documented config/default path.
- **What was not tested**: A screenshot/recording of the browser-rendered bubble was not captured because the proof focuses on the exact Control UI request/projection boundary that caused the bug. Unit/UI tests and build were run separately.

## Testing

- `../node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` from `ui/`
- `../node_modules/.bin/vite build` from `ui/`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations should be rejected at registry assembly; trusted setup-entry channel registration failures should not abort sibling plugin loading. - Real environment tested: local Linux repo checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `717003aaffec3d41792424079a52beaf2bd92a5c`; PR head `55811ad9edce27d49decb99c7564fba5c4138fb3`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - `pnpm docs:list` - `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/channel-plugin-resolution.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/discovery.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/channels.add.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm build` - `pnpm check:changed` - Evidence after fix: `src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts` passes 16/16 after exposing `listRawChannelPluginCatalogEntries` through the same mock/actual fallback as the old catalog helper; the focused loader/registry/catalog/channel setup tests pass after the rebase; `pnpm build` and the final `pnpm check:changed` pass; `CHANGELOG.md` and stale transcripts tool-display metadata are no longer in the final branch diff. - Observed result after fix: disabled workspace shadows no longer execute through scoped setup-only loads; trusted workspace overrides remain available; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected as a registry backstop; malformed trusted setup-entry channel registration records a diagnostic and sibling plugins keep loading. - What was not tested: live channel setup against a real external workspace plugin outside the loader test harness. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same minimal setup-only repro failed on latest `origin/main` before this branch because the marker file was created during scoped setup-only load. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: the scoped setup-only loader path treated explicit `onlyPluginIds` as sufficient to load a disabled workspace channel plugin, so workspace trust was bypassed below the trusted-catalog call sites. - Missing detection / guardrail: registry assembly still accepted channel registration from disabled workspace plugins if a lower-level caller imported one. - Complementary #85774 root cause: the setup-entry loader loop caught surrounding registration failures, but `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)` itself was unguarded, so one throwing trusted channel registration could abort sibling plugin loading. - Contributing context: latest `main` already absorbed the higher-level `channels add` / trusted-catalog call-site fixes from #64154, but not this lower-level loader/registry backstop or the setup-entry error-isolation guard. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`, `src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts`, `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts`, and existing channel setup/add tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: explicit scoped setup-only loads must reject disabled workspace channel plugins but keep trusted workspace channel plugins available; target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`
